source_paper_title: Positivstellensätze and Moment Problems with Universal Quantifiers
context:
Section 7 (Conclusions and Discussions), page 26

Our work leads to many intriguing questions to explore in the future. For instance, without assuming archimedeanness, is there a preordering version of Theorem 3.4? Equivalently, does there exist a clean algebraic reformulation of the compactness (or emptiness) of the set \(K\) given with a universal quantifier as in (1.1)?
title: Preordering (Schm"udgen-type) Positivstellensatz for universally quantified sets without archimedeanness
latex:
Let \(n,m,s\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(Q\subseteq\mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set and let \(g_1,\dots,g_s\in\mathbb{R}[x,y]\) be polynomials in variables \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) and \(y=(y_1,\dots,y_m)\in\mathbb{R}^m\). Define the universally quantified feasible set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \text{for all }y\in Q\text{ and all }j\in\{1,\dots,s\}\}.
\]
Let \(\nu\) be a finite nonnegative Borel measure on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\operatorname{supp}(\nu)=Q\) and satisfying the multivariate Carleman condition
\[
\sum_{d=0}^{\infty}\Big(\int_{\mathbb{R}^m} y_j^{2d}\,d\nu(y)\Big)^{-\!1/(2d)}=\infty\quad\text{for each }j\in\{1,\dots,m\}.
\]
Define the cone (quadratic module)
\[
\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]:=\Big\{\sum_{j=0}^s\int_{\mathbb{R}^m}\tau_j(x,y)\,g_j(x,y)\,d\nu(y):\ \tau_j\in\Sigma^2[x,y]\Big\},\quad g_0:=1,
\]
where \(\Sigma^2[x,y]\) denotes the cone of sum-of-squares polynomials in \((x,y)\).

Problem: Construct an appropriate \emph{preordering} cone \(\mathrm{PO}[g,\nu]\subseteq\mathbb{R}[x]\) (i.e., an algebraic cone generated by finitely many expressions built from the constraints \(g_1,\dots,g_s\) and the measure \(\nu\), analogous to the classical Schm\"udgen preordering) such that the following implication holds \emph{without any archimedeanness assumption} on \(\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]\):
- for every polynomial \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\), if \(f(x)>0\) for all \(x\in K\), then \(f\in \mathrm{PO}[g,\nu]\).

Equivalently, determine whether there exists a "clean" algebraic certificate, formulated purely in terms of \(g\) and \(\nu\), that characterizes compactness (or emptiness) of \(K\) when \(K\) is defined by universal quantifiers as above, paralleling the role of preorderings and the Krivine--Stengle Positivstellensatz for basic closed semialgebraic sets.
open_problem_quote: Our work leads to many intriguing questions to explore in the future. For instance, without assuming archimedeanness, is there a preordering version of Theorem 3.4? Equivalently, does there exist a clean algebraic reformulation of the compactness (or emptiness) of the set \(K\) given with a universal quantifier as in (1.1)?

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
